name: Braised Lettuces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Trim and discard the outer leaves from the escarole, leaving the lighter green center. Cut in half from root to tip.
3. Trim and discard any discolored outer leaves of endive and Boston lettuce and cut them in half from root to tip.
4. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the lettuces and cook until golden on both sides. If the lettuces don't fit in the skillet, perform this operation in batches.
5. Add garlic, coriander, thyme, rosemary, water, and vinegar to the skillet. Cover, place in the oven, and cook for 15 minutes.
6. Transfer the skillet to the stove top and remove the lettuces. Let cool on a plate.
7. Press the lettuces and add any braising liquid to the skillet. Add the cream and cook until the liquid becomes saucelike.
8. Pour into a bowl and let cool. To serve, cut halves of Boston lettuce and escarole in half. Arrange all lettuces on a platter and pour the sauce over the top.
